SEARCH
Package minum.web.http2

Class Http2Frame

java.lang.Object
minum.web.http2.Http2Frame
Direct Known Subclasses:
Http2HeaderFrame
public abstract class Http2Frame extends Object
All frames begin with a fixed 9-octet header followed by a variable-length frame payload. See RFC 9113 section 4.1 - Frame format 
 
 HTTP Frame {
   Length (24),
   Type (8),

   Flags (8),

   Reserved (1),
   Stream Identifier (31),

   Frame Payload (..),
 }
See the fields for further details.

    static Http2Frame
    decode(ByteArrayInputStream in)
    Given an input stream of bytes, either returns a value type of HTTP2 frame, or returns a null (or an exception if it's really broken)

    Given a Request, return a list of Http2Frame making up the serialized set of data we'll put on the wire

  • Constructor Details

    • Http2Frame

      public Http2Frame(int length, Http2FrameType type, byte flags, int streamIdentifier, byte[] payload)

  • Method Details

    • length

      public int length()
      Length: The length of the frame payload expressed as an unsigned 24-bit integer in units of octets. Values greater than 2^14 (16,384) MUST NOT be sent unless the receiver has set a larger value for SETTINGS_MAX_FRAME_SIZE. The 9 octets of the frame header are not included in this value.

    • type

      public Http2FrameType type()
      Type: The 8-bit type of the frame. The frame type determines the format and semantics of the frame. Frames defined in this document are listed in Section 6. Implementations MUST ignore and discard frames of unknown types.

    • flags

      public byte flags()
      Flags: An 8-bit field reserved for boolean flags specific to the frame type. Flags are assigned semantics specific to the indicated frame type. Unused flags are those that have no defined semantics for a particular frame type. Unused flags MUST be ignored on receipt and MUST be left unset (0x00) when sending.

    • streamIdentifier

      public int streamIdentifier()
      Stream Identifier: A stream identifier (see Section 5.1.1) expressed as an unsigned 31-bit integer. The value 0x00 is reserved for frames that are associated with the connection as a whole as opposed to an individual stream.

    • encode

      public static List<Http2Frame> encode(Request r, String scheme, String host)
      Given a Request, return a list of Http2Frame making up the serialized set of data we'll put on the wire
      Parameters:
      scheme - "http" or "https"
      host - the hostname of the server

    • decode

      public static Http2Frame decode(ByteArrayInputStream in)
      Given an input stream of bytes, either returns a value type of HTTP2 frame, or returns a null (or an exception if it's really broken)

    • payload

      public byte[] payload()
      The structure and content of the frame payload are dependent entirely on the frame type.

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Overrides:
